reasoning: The Lean hypothesis `∀ v : V, ∃ c : F, v ∈ eigenspace S c` expresses "for every v there exists a scalar c with S(v) = c·v" (via `mem_eigenspace_iff`). While Axler defines eigenvectors as nonzero, the case v = 0 is trivially satisfied (0 ∈ eigenspace S c for any c), so the hypothesis is equivalent to "every nonzero v is an eigenvector of S" — matching the informal claim. The conclusion `∃ c : F, S = c • LinearMap.id` expresses "S equals c times the identity operator", which is exactly "S is a scalar multiple of the identity". The ambient types (AddCommGroup V, Field F, Module F V, S : End F V) correctly model a linear operator on a vector space. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
